# hello-world-api translation

Translate the Flask source API in `src/` into a Java Spring Boot API in `dst/`.

The Java service must be a drop-in replacement:

- Listen on the port from the `SERVER_PORT` environment variable.
- Serve `GET /`.
- Return JSON with a `message` field.
- Without `name`, return `{"message":"Hello World!"}`.
- With `name`, trim leading/trailing spaces and return `{"message":"Hello <name>!"}`.

Put all target implementation files under `dst/`. The expected build command is:

```sh
mvn clean package -DskipTests
```

The expected run command is:

```sh
SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar
```

text: The API surface is minimal: one `GET /` route, JSON `{ "message": ... }`, `name` query param trimmed with blank values falling back to `"World"`, and port sourced from `SERVER_PORT`. I’m setting up a small Spring Boot app in `dst/` with that exact behavior and Maven packaging so the requested build and run commands work unchanged.
I’m creating the Maven project and Java sources now. After that I’ll build and run the jar locally to verify both the port binding and the response payloads.

The directory structure is in place. Next I’m adding the Spring Boot application, controller, and Maven config with the runtime port wired to `SERVER_PORT` and a default of `3000` to match the Flask entrypoint.
The implementation is in place. I’m building it with Maven now, then I’ll start the jar on port `3000` and hit both the default and trimmed-name cases to confirm the HTTP behavior.

Maven is still resolving and compiling. I’ve also confirmed the project only contains the new target files under `dst/`; once the build finishes I’ll run the packaged jar and verify the responses directly.

text:
Implemented a direct Spring Boot replacement under `dst/` with the same API behavior as the Flask app. The app reads `SERVER_PORT` via [application.properties](/home/user/workspace/dst/src/main/resources/application.properties:1), serves `GET /` from [HelloController.java](/home/user/workspace/dst/src/main/java/com/example/helloworld/HelloController.java:1), trims the optional `name` query param, and falls back to `"World"` for blank or missing values.

Build and runtime verification both succeeded from `dst/`: `mvn clean package -DskipTests` completed successfully, and `SERVER_PORT=3000 java -jar target/*.jar` served `{"message":"Hello World!"}` at `/` and `{"message":"Hello Alice!"}` at `/?name=%20%20Alice%20%20`. The Maven project entrypoint is [pom.xml](/home/user/workspace/dst/pom.xml:1) with the application bootstrap in [HelloWorldApplication.java](/home/user/workspace/dst/src/main/java/com/example/helloworld/HelloWorldApplication.java:1).
